hypothesis: If trained diffusion-based generative models (CDVAE, GemsDiff, DiffCSP) are applied to preprocessed MP/OQMD data, then they will produce novel crystal structures that are more chemically valid and thermodynamically stable—exhibiting higher interatomic-distance validity, lower RMSD after DFT relaxation, lower energy above the hull / higher SUN scores, and formation-energy and density distributions closer to the training data—than structures produced by controlled perturbations of known materials.
